scoring_hand_only.title: Only cards in the scoring poker hand score
scoring_hand_only.rule: Balatro identifies the best poker hand among the played cards, and only the cards forming that poker hand are scored. Extra played cards outside that subset (kickers) do not add their chips and do not trigger their on-score card effects (editions, seals, on-scored enhancements). Splash is the exception that lets every played card score.
source: Balatro Wiki — Guide: Activation Sequence: https://balatrowiki.org/w/Guide:_Activation_Sequence
Balatro Wiki — Poker hands (only relevant cards are scored): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Poker_hands

held_in_hand_effects.title: Held-in-hand effects come from cards left in hand
held_in_hand_effects.rule: After scoring the played cards, the game separately evaluates cards still held in hand for held-in-hand effects (e.g. Steel Card's X1.5 Mult, Baron's per-King X1.5, Raised Fist). This is why Steel cards are usually kept in hand rather than played.
source: Balatro Wiki — Guide: Activation Sequence (Held in hand abilities): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Guide:_Activation_Sequence

glass_scores_and_breaks_only_when_scorin.title: Glass value and break risk are tied to scoring cards
glass_scores_and_breaks_only_when_scorin.rule: Glass cards give X2 Mult when scored and have a 1-in-4 chance to destroy themselves after all scoring is finished. Both the X2 and the shatter apply only to cards in the scoring hand, not to extra played kickers.
source: Balatro Wiki — Card modifiers#Enhancements (Glass Card): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Card_modifiers#Enhancements
Balatro Wiki — Guide: Activation Sequence: https://balatrowiki.org/w/Guide:_Activation_Sequence

the_fool_copies_last_tarot_or_planet.title: The Fool copies the last Tarot or Planet used
the_fool_copies_last_tarot_or_planet.rule: The Fool creates a copy of the most recent Tarot or Planet card used during the run (The Fool itself excluded). It is only usable when such a recorded card exists.
source: Balatro Wiki — Tarot cards (The Fool): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Tarot_cards

death_uses_rightmost_selected_card_as_ta.title: Death converts the left selected card into a copy of the right
death_uses_rightmost_selected_card_as_ta.rule: Death selects 2 cards and converts the left selected card into a copy of the right selected card (you can drag to rearrange the selection before using it).
source: Balatro Wiki — Tarot cards (Death): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Tarot_cards

native_rank_and_suit_sort.title: Rank and suit sorting should use the game's native hand sort
native_rank_and_suit_sort.rule: Balatro's Sort by Rank button orders hand cards by rank (Ace high) descending; Sort by Suit orders by suit then rank. BlindDeck's sort endpoint invokes the game's native sort, so prefer `bot.ps1 sort` over manually rearranging cards.
source: Balatro Wiki — Card Ranks (Ace scores high, rank ordering): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Card_Ranks

selling_can_be_relevant_during_pack_choi.title: Selling can free slots before choosing a booster reward
selling_can_be_relevant_during_pack_choi.rule: You can sell a Joker or consumable to free a slot before choosing a booster pack reward, which is useful when your Joker or consumable slots are full and the pack offers a card you want to keep.
source: Balatro Wiki — Jokers (held in slots until sold or destroyed): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Jokers
Balatro Wiki — Booster Packs (use consumables while in a pack): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Booster_Packs

cartomancer_needs_consumable_space.title: Cartomancer needs consumable capacity
cartomancer_needs_consumable_space.rule: Cartomancer creates a Tarot card when a Blind is selected, but only if there is room in your consumable area — the 'Create' action always requires a free slot (Perkeo's Negative copies are the sole exception).
source: Balatro Wiki — Jokers (Create requires room): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Jokers
Balatro Wiki — Tarot cards (Cartomancer, "room"): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Tarot_cards

face_cards_are_jack_queen_king_unless_mo.title: Face cards are Jack, Queen, King unless modified
face_cards_are_jack_queen_king_unless_mo.rule: Face cards are Jacks, Queens, and Kings (ranks 11-13), each worth 10 chips when scored. Pareidolia makes every card count as a face card. Ace (rank 14, worth 11 chips) is not a face card by default.
source: Balatro Wiki — Card Ranks (Face cards = J/Q/K; Pareidolia): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Card_Ranks

jokers_are_limited_shared_build_slots.title: Jokers are limited shared build slots
jokers_are_limited_shared_build_slots.rule: Jokers are the main build-defining cards and occupy limited Joker slots (5 by default; some decks change this). The Negative edition adds +1 Joker slot, while Foil, Holographic, and Polychrome add score value instead.
source: Balatro Wiki — Jokers (5 Joker slots; editions): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Jokers

booster_pack_rewards_are_free_picks.title: Booster pack contents are free picks after the pack is opened
booster_pack_rewards_are_free_picks.rule: After a booster pack is opened, the cards shown are choices from the already-paid pack reward, not shop offers that require paying their printed buy price again. The player can pick from them or skip at any point.
source: Balatro Wiki — Booster Packs (open immediately, choose or skip): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Booster_Packs

tags_are_skip_rewards.title: Tags are rewards for skipping the shown Blind
tags_are_skip_rewards.rule: Tags are rewards for skipping a Small Blind or Big Blind. Skipping adds the displayed tag, marks the current blind as skipped, and advances to the next blind; a tag may trigger immediately or wait until a later condition is met (e.g. the next Shop).
source: Balatro Wiki — Tags (rewards for skipping a Small or Big Blind): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Tags

scoring_formula.title: Final score = Chips × Mult, built up in fixed phases (left to right)
scoring_formula.rule: A played hand's final score is Chips × Mult. The running Chips and Mult are built in order: (1) add the poker hand type's base Chips and base Mult (from its current level — check with `bot.ps1 query hands`); (2) for each SCORING card (left to right) add its card chips plus any +chips enhancement, then trigger its on-score effects (editions/seals, e.g. Gold seal +$3, Red seal retrigger); (3) trigger Jokers left to right, each applying +Chips, +Mult, or XMult to the running totals; (4) final score = Chips × Mult. XMult multiplies the Mult accumulated so far, so it is strongest AFTER +Mult has been stacked.
source: Balatro Wiki — Guide: Activation Sequence: https://balatrowiki.org/w/Guide:_Activation_Sequence

kickers_do_not_score.title: Only the cards forming the poker hand type score — kickers add no chips
kickers_do_not_score.rule: Balatro selects the best poker hand among the played cards and only that subset scores. Cards outside that subset (kickers) do NOT add their chips and do NOT trigger on-score card effects. Example: a 5-card play scored as Three of a Kind scores only the 3 matching cards' chips, not the 2 kickers — so 3×King (10 each) + base 30 = 60 chips × 3 mult = 180, NOT the sum of all 5 cards. This is the most common score-estimate mistake. (Reinforces scoring_hand_only with the kicker consequence.)
source: Balatro Wiki — Poker hands (only relevant cards are scored): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Poker_hands
Balatro Wiki — Guide: Activation Sequence: https://balatrowiki.org/w/Guide:_Activation_Sequence

card_chip_values.title: Card chip values: Ace 11, 2-10 face value, J/Q/K 10
card_chip_values.rule: When a scoring card adds its chips: Ace = 11, 2-10 = face value, Jack/Queen/King = 10. These are the base chip contributions before enhancements/editions. Use these to estimate the card-chip portion of a hand before playing (only counting the scoring subset — see kickers_do_not_score).
source: Balatro Wiki — Card Ranks (Ace 11, face cards 10, number cards face value): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Card_Ranks

hand_base_values_level_1.title: Poker hand base Chips/Mult at level 1 (use query hands for current levels)
hand_base_values_level_1.rule: Base Chips/Mult at level 1: High Card 5/1, Pair 10/2, Two Pair 20/2, Three of a Kind 30/3, Straight 30/4, Flush 35/4, Full House 40/4, Four of a Kind 60/7, Straight Flush 100/8, Five of a Kind 120/12, Flush House 140/14, Flush Five 160/16. Planet cards permanently raise a hand's level (increasing its base Chips and Mult) for the rest of the run. ALWAYS check the current level with `bot.ps1 query hands` before estimating — leveled-up hands have much higher base values than these level-1 figures.
source: Balatro Wiki — Poker hands (Base Scoring per hand at level 1): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Poker_hands

additive_before_multiplicative_mult.title: Place +Mult Jokers/cards to the LEFT of XMult effects
additive_before_multiplicative_mult.rule: Because XMult multiplies the Mult accumulated so far, +Mult should trigger BEFORE XMult for a higher score. Order Jokers left-to-right so +Chips/+Mult Jokers are left of XMult Jokers. Same for played cards: put +Mult cards (e.g. Mult enhancement) left of XMult cards (e.g. Glass, Polychrome). Reverse the order only if you intentionally want a LOWER score (e.g. to feed scaling Jokers). Example: 40 chips, base mult 4, +4 Mult Joker and X2 Ramen → Joker left of Ramen = 40×((4+4)×2)=640; reversed = 40×((4×2)+4)=480.
source: Balatro Wiki — Guide: Activation Sequence (Additive Mult - Multiplicative Mult Order): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Guide:_Activation_Sequence

edition_values.title: Edition effects: Foil +50 chips, Holographic +10 mult, Polychrome X1.5 mult, Negative +1 slot
edition_values.rule: Editions on Jokers/playing cards: Foil = +50 Chips (added when the card scores / directly before the Joker is reached during scoring); Holographic = +10 Mult; Polychrome = X1.5 Mult (directly after the Joker is reached); Negative = +1 Joker or Consumable slot. These stack with the card's normal effect. Editions on playing cards apply during card scoring (phase 2).
source: Balatro Wiki — Card modifiers#Editions: https://balatrowiki.org/w/Card_modifiers#Editions
Balatro Wiki — Jokers (Editions): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Jokers

enhancement_scoring.title: Playing-card enhancements and when they trigger (Bonus/Mult/Glass/Steel/Stone/Gold/Lucky/Wild)
enhancement_scoring.rule: There are 8 playing-card enhancements: Bonus = +30 Chips when scored; Mult = +4 Mult when scored; Glass = X2 Mult when scored (1-in-4 chance to destroy itself after scoring); Steel = X1.5 Mult while HELD in hand — do NOT play Steel cards, they score as a normal card and lose the Steel bonus; Stone = +50 Chips when scored, has no rank/suit but always scores; Gold = +$3 if held in hand at end of round (do NOT need to be played); Lucky = when scored, a 1-in-5 chance for +20 Mult and a separate 1-in-15 chance for +$20 (both can trigger the same score); Wild = all suits simultaneously, rank unchanged (see wild_card_enhancement). On-score enhancements only apply to cards in the scoring hand (see kickers_do_not_score); Steel and Gold are the exceptions, applying to held cards instead.
source: Balatro Wiki — Card modifiers#Enhancements (8 enhancements and exact values): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Card_modifiers#Enhancements
Balatro Wiki — Guide: Activation Sequence: https://balatrowiki.org/w/Guide:_Activation_Sequence

wild_card_enhancement.title: Wild Card enhancement: all suits, rank unchanged
wild_card_enhancement.rule: Wild (The Lovers, packs, Illusion shop) keeps the card's printed rank — it is NOT a wildcard rank. A Queen Wild cannot pair with an Ace; only with another Queen (or rank-changing effects like Strength). For suits: Wild counts as Spades, Hearts, Clubs, and Diamonds simultaneously — helping Flushes, Straights, Straight Flushes, and most suit-based Jokers (Sinful, Rough Gem, Ancient, etc.). Exception: logic that needs one card per suit (Flower Pot, Seeing Double) assigns each Wild to exactly ONE suit at a time — a single Wild cannot satisfy multiple suit slots alone. When debuffed, Wild loses the enhancement and reverts to its original suit only. Unlike Stone (no rank/suit), Wild has normal rank chip value. Multiple Wilds in one play can upgrade hand tier (e.g. five Wilds → Flush Five over Five of a Kind). Wild cards do not count toward deck unlock tallies like "30 Diamonds in deck".
source: Balatro Wiki — Wild cards: https://balatrowiki.org/w/Wild_cards
Balatro Wiki — Card modifiers#Enhancements: https://balatrowiki.org/w/Card_modifiers#Enhancements
Balatro Wiki — Card Suits: https://balatrowiki.org/w/Card_Suits

seal_effects.title: Seal effects: Gold +$3 on score, Red retrigger, Blue creates Planet on score, Purple creates Tarot on discard
seal_effects.rule: Card seals (a card can have at most one seal): Gold seal = +$3 when the card is played and scores; Red seal = retriggers the card's scoring effect (scores twice); Blue seal = when the card is held in hand at end of round, creates the Planet card matching the final poker hand played that round (must have room); Purple seal = creates a Tarot card when the card is discarded (must have room). Seals only trigger for scoring cards (Gold/Red/Blue) or discarded cards (Purple), and Blue/Purple require free consumable slot space.
source: Balatro Wiki — Card modifiers#Seals (Gold $3, Red retrigger, Blue planet, Purple Tarot on discard): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Card_modifiers#Seals

plasma_deck_balances_chips_and_mult.title: Plasma Deck averages Chips and Mult (never reduces the final score)
plasma_deck_balances_chips_and_mult.rule: On the Plasma Deck, after computing Chips and Mult they are balanced: both become (Chips + Mult) / 2. By AM-GM the product never decreases, so balancing never reduces the final score — it rewards balancing chip and mult investments rather than stacking one. Score estimates on Plasma should use the averaged values.
source: Balatro Wiki — Guide: Activation Sequence (Plasma Deck balance): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Guide:_Activation_Sequence

planet_cards_level_up_hands.title: Planet consumables permanently level up one poker hand type
planet_cards_level_up_hands.rule: Each Planet card permanently raises the level of one specific poker hand type for the rest of the run, increasing that hand's base Chips and base Mult. The per-level increment is shown in Run Info → Poker Hands. Planet cards are the primary way to scale a chosen hand direction (e.g. leveling Flushes for a Flush build). Use `bot.ps1 query hands` to see the current level/chips/mult of every hand.
source: Balatro Wiki — Planet cards (level up a poker hand for the run): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Planet_cards
Balatro Wiki — Poker hands (level via Planet cards): https://balatrowiki.org/w/Poker_hands

ankh_random_joker.title: Ankh / Hex / Ectoplasm pick a random joker (not API-targetable)
ankh_random_joker.rule: Ankh and Hex require at least one joker but copy or Polychrome a RANDOM joker and destroy the rest. Ectoplasm adds Negative to a random joker. BlindDeck `pack` trailing indices only highlight HAND cards — they do not select a joker slot. `rearrange jokers` changes scoring order only; it does not aim these effects. To guarantee Ankh/Hex keeps a specific joker, sell every other joker first so only the keeper remains.
source: Balatro Wiki — Ankh: https://balatrowiki.org/w/Ankh
Balatro Wiki — Hex: https://balatrowiki.org/w/Hex
Balatro Wiki — Ectoplasm: https://balatrowiki.org/w/Ectoplasm
